So today, many financial companies are very quickly making a demo. It's nothing for me. Just like in 2017, there were a lot of data companies in China at that time. It's the same as today's financial. Of course, there are more financials. In fact, it is. It doesn't mean that these technologies can ultimately see the true value. So it's just a simple comparison. Because such a comparison is not good.

Q：你看人的标准变了吗？人才密度还是人才潜力？

You chose the robot's manager. Has it had any impact on your later selection standards? Do you think this is a usable method?

Because of what you said, I would think that Musk likes to use talented people. He doesn't like to use those expert-like people. I think maybe in Tesla's, I think it's very difficult to bring it to most countries, cities, or industries.

I think LC is very focused on the density of talent. I think it's the potential of talent. It basically uses the best people. For example, from the end of last year to the beginning of this year, we have graduates like Dr. Shanqing. They are only in one department and they are nearly 80. They are all very expensive. But we are all willing. You need to have the ability to understand such a group of people. You need to be willing to support their long-term exploration. You need to believe that these young people or girls can create miracles.

I think it's the potential of talent. He thinks it's the density of talent. I think 要用超級聰明的人去做超級困難的事情, 而不是說用非常清晰的方向或者流程或者工具, I think it's very right at some point.

十四、为什么必须是人形机器人

At that time, you had to bet on a humanoid robot. What was your reason for choosing it in 2023? Why must it be a humanoid robot?

I think it's because every company has chosen it. I think I'm very happy that I'm the same as my team LC. LC is crazier than me. He kept telling me that he wanted to recruit people, not to recruit robots.

Most people think of robots as commercial products. But I don't think he thought about the robots in this society, in this universe. 参与感或者跟我们每个人的情绪价值的连接度 就像2014年很多人觉得智能汽车的智能是没有价值的。 我觉得站在今天也有很多人 第一不相信通用汽车会到来 第二也根本不愿意看到通用汽车也许在20年50年以后会跟人类的 各个领域强相关。

今天刚才我们所讨论的数字AI, 只是辅助人类的 可能以三个为主的 AI 更长寿 更开心。 第二个 我觉得在物理AI上 机器人对老人的影响度非常大。 老人很有可能把机器人作为他唯一的依赖。 我觉得至少有人会这样思考吧。 我觉得小朋友机器人的思考道路 跟很多公司是不一样的。

十五、那场意外：Iron 被怀疑藏人的那一晚

去年那波爆炸性的讨论 有出乎你的意料吗 你当时还挺委屈的。

对 我们是没有想到的 我们实际上想法非常的技术化。 因为去年那款机器人是我们前年 第一季度开始做的。 对我们来说,它只是我们的一个中间版本。

那这个中间版本,我们只是想认证, 如果一个机器人有能这么多的关节, 像我们的关节还是比能少很多, 能有200多个关节。 and there are muscles and skin. I can tell you that many of the muscles we see today, their external movements, if they have muscles and skin, their movements will change and shrink significantly. Because this is a completely different thing.

In another way, we just want to explore if in this situation, can enter our lives or our work more easily.

例子：金毛 vs 机器狗

Let me give you an example. For example, we used to ride a horse or a dog before. No matter what the horse or dog is, it can't enter your room. If you let a 7-foot-1 to 1.2-meter-long hole enter your room, your room will soon have a lot of problems. For example, it can't hang its head on the bedside table.

如果你家裡有一隻金毛, 牠在掉頭的時候, 牠的尾巴, 牠會往上動一下, 牠會抬起, 而且牠會刮到牆跟你的床。 你不會覺得牠會受傷, 你也不會覺得你的床會受傷。 但是一隻機器狗, 牠百分之百會讓你們兩個都覺得受傷。

那狗做小一點呢? 如果牠做得非常的小, 牠的能力級就非常的差。 It is light. Another problem is that it has a very short battery life. This is the first one.

The second one, I will say one more. If it is a twin-engine, with armor, and a size of 1.8 meters. Today, even if you are with it, you are its designer, you are not willing to be one centimeter away from it. It feels suppressed. You think it may be dangerous. You think it may be very hot. You may think he has electricity in his body. You may think he is dirty. Anyway, for some reason, you will keep a distance from him.

If even we adults do this, what will the elderly and children do? How should it solve the safety and legal problems in society? An industrial-based robot can make such a logic. Because it's not in the family or business itself. So many people go to industrial-based robots. He said I avoided the ABC problem. What I just said is the ABC problem. But industrial-based robots have triggered a new problem. Is that the ability to control is very strong. It will introduce a new problem.

I still look back. In the future, if robots enter the human society, they will have a lot of value in many areas, whether it's basic blue or white. They will definitely enter our lives. So we have chosen a very difficult path. So I myself think that many Chinese companies, whether in quality and safety, including recent sports conferences, we can see that just a little management will lead to such an interesting phenomenon. It's still in the mind of the boss to think logically. Why not do some mediation?

I think we are already in the middle stage. Our middle stage is that if you want to bring the body to the human world, we are already choosing the easiest middle stage. How tall should this person be? Look at our current generation of body, it's about 1.69 to 1.70 meters. It has to be comfortable for both men and women. Yes, and it can wear clothes, it can even have hair, but it can't have its own face. It has to be a certain distance from humans.

Q：为什么不能有面孔？

Why can't it have its own face?

There are some effects of the horror bone, and some other effects of law and social studies. So in fact, the robot that everyone is seeing now and the robot that the child is doing still have a big difference. I think we'll all wait until the second half of this year. I look forward to a very deep thinking, a very simple middle position, and then use the resources of the whole company, the resources of the whole small company to do a good job.

十六、剪开机器人的腿：那一晚的决断

At the press conference last year, you said you were very troubled. Do you want to prove that the truth is in it? Do you want to show everyone? Why are you troubled? What are you troubled about?

I'm not troubled. My team is very troubled. I think I should say it right away. My team said, the more you say it, the more people will think you have a problem. Because you will think that the pro is pro, the left is left. This is one kind of team view.

The second kind of team view is that we can observe it again and wait for 24 hours. We can see if it's worth it. I can't wait for hours. I've been discussing this in China and around the world. It's spreading very fast. I said, after 24 hours, the bullet may have flown somewhere. It's very simple. Because we know that the people who are developing and using it are not the same people.

To be honest, the same goes for the other successor. His management was problematic. He was very hot. But he was just a milestone in our team. We knew that the later version would be great. So my dilemma was how to make the internal team be motivated. Because many people inside felt that they could not say anything. Because we knew it ourselves. But in fact, 99.99% of the world's population, they can't get in touch with it.

I remember that night, I gave our team a call. I said, "Tomorrow morning, you guys come up with an idea for me. I'm going to tell everyone that this is a real robot, not a human hiding inside." They were forced out by me.

Q：为什么选择剪腿？

Why did you choose to cut it?

Because there are many people online. There are various ways. If you are A, you must be because of this reason. It's a real person. If you are B, it's probably because of that reason. We think the easiest way is to cut his legs. and left leg. Because everyone can see his left leg. Because he walks from left to right. The left leg is the easiest to see. This is a real robot. So we did such a move.

Q：那波舆论带来什么信号？

What kind of signal did the explosive public opinion bring you last year? In the development of robots.

I think there are many interesting results. We later went to see many social media in China. Their understanding of the value of this robot. Most young people they all want to get into families and help them with their work.

I know of some people who don't post on social media. Men and women of middle age and above. Even older than me. Many people are considering what use this technology will have when they get older. I think this is very different. It's not as big a concept as before. It doesn't have as much data. I think this is all about different perspectives.

Has it changed the way you work with robots?

I think it has. In fact, many very good people have joined the mobile industry. Mobile is very different from car business. I can't share it with you now. I think in three or four years, I think it's very valuable.

十七、机器人公司的胜率与"非人形"路线

I still want to say that the challenge of this matter is very interesting for every company, including ourselves, but also a very big challenge. Today, there are too many robot companies. I think it's far more than the number of new cars that were built when you built the car. When I built the car, there were more than 300 cars. But I feel like there are more than 100 cars. Maybe many of them are too many. I suspect. I think there are more than 200 robot companies that have been established. Actually, it may be double than the car company of that year.

But I still want to say that the machine is different from the car. Because the classification in the car may be used car. For example, new car, commercial car, or special car, or micro car. You see, this is the classification.

I think the classification of the machine is not only seven or eight. There may be countless. 比如说做医疗的机器人, 机器人的体系里面做专门的货运的机器人, 或者货检的机器人, 我觉得都是可能有, 而且机器人的逻辑里面有一个非常有趣的特点, 我认为机器人有很多机器人不需要是能行的, 这个我也很认同, 只是说我们选了一个最像能的能行机器人, 我们选了一条这个道路。

So on this road, we are still moving forward. But there are many roads that I think can also go out of the gap and business. So from another perspective, I think today's mobile companies, I myself think if you go through the general human-powered machine, 99.99% will die. But if you go through all kinds of different machines, I think there may be a lot of solutions in this world.

Oh, then the victory rate of robots is quite high.

I think it's higher than the used cars. That's my opinion.

Q：那为什么选最难的路？

Then why did you choose the most difficult path?

What do you want to do? What do you have the ability to do? What do you think you should do? And what do you not do? And how do you think you can achieve the best probability? You put these five combination of abilities, isn't it your enterprise or product unit?

In your opinion, who is your opponent in the humanoid robot?

I think there is no opponent in the humanoid robot. It's all about yourself. Because every robot company is most important to make their own capabilities the strongest. Their own capabilities are the foundation, and then the sound. Then the entire system, including technology, products, and products' system capabilities. Then the engineering.

十八、机器人的运动控制：还停留在 T 型车时代

I think last year, your robot was very successful because it was very attractive. What are the key technical benefits behind this? And what time did you spend on it?

I can talk about a small part. For example, I think most people underestimate the movement control of robots. In fact, movement control in the automotive industry is something that many people have felt solved in the past 100 years. So many car companies go to make cars, they all buy 随随随的运动控制的能力 去进行组合基层, 基层在过去的时间 可能来自于2018年 某些开源的运动控制 NPC等等这样的一些事情。 所以他们为什么看起来 有很多的共识性。

我觉得最终的一个基层 真正要做好的, 它应该完全 比汽车的下下代还要能够做得更好。 因为汽车实际上是由不同的控制域去做单点控制, 它的整体上是没有全协同的。 它只是说它把边界做得极好, 所以它把它分工能够分好, 你做A,我做B,我们两个有清晰的边界。

但实际上,比如说举一个例子, 如果一个自动驾驶的汽车, 左轮胎是雪地,右轮胎是草地的情况下, 要转一个47度5的弯,该怎么转? 这个对人都很难, 因为你左边是雪地,右边是草地的粘着力, 你整个四轮的控制,整个运动的平衡, 整个食盐的管理, 如果刚好转弯的时候, 前面有车,左边有人, 是极其容易滑车就追尾上去了。

So I think the driving control of cars today is very good, but it is far from being fully capable. There is still a lot of room for improvement. If we look at the machine, I don't know how many years ago it was, maybe in the 1930s or 1920s. Maybe it was the era of T-shaped cars. Do you have T-shaped cars today? I don't think there are T-shaped cars in the machine. But I think from the perspective of the underlying movement control, it may still belong to that generation.

Back to your question just now, I think it's extremely difficult for you to control the movement of a full AI combination. For example, today you see all the machines, they either walk or fight. Their two movement controls may have to be adjusted. Then you say you have five movement controls, and that's it. You have to have, in fact, for a person, a person has more than 200 joints, its combination is infinite. Just like I have two fists now. And it also has facial expressions, hands, eyes, feet, your soul and emotional system.

So I think that we are actually in the field of sports control, we are very eager to think like people, how can all sports be controlled by AI, not by software. 而且这个控制如何能做到平衡, 就是像人的本能一样。 当一个人学会走路 学会跑步 他是有本能能力的。 当他看到一个地面 可能是有一个 it will even try to walk to feel the friction and the whole body. It may be a two-way road with both open and cold water.

So in the infrastructure field, we have to think about this kind of situation. So I think that the sports control of Xiaopeng may be a bit like the lower-down generation of cars. So this is also why we think that the absolute majority of the inheritance to the cars that were controlled by sports. So he can only use software to integrate it. Naturally, it's hard to make a good robot.

十九、机器人对小鹏的三重意义

What does the robot mean to Xiao Peng? Is it his distant future?

I don't think so. We think there are three lines in Xiao Peng's new career.

第一线：汽车。 The first line is the car. He wants to make the car a complete intelligent body.

第二线：机器人。 The second line is the robot. The robot itself is a self-driving car. Although the car still needs to prove that it can be used for 10 years, it can prove that the software can withstand 50%. But I don't think the robot needs to prove it. If a robot has poor software capabilities, you basically won't want it. Of course, if the hardware is poor, you won't use it.

第三线：全球化。 I think the third line of reference is that we are globalized.

Although the robot is very different from the car, most people ignore it. Once the robot has the ability to scale up, its speed of scaling up will be far beyond the speed of the car. In the past 100 years, cars have been gradually scaled down. Because they have roads to build, and the law of transportation to be built. Third, mass production is very difficult.

For example, you made a very good car today, and sold 100,000 cars a day. Sorry, you can't make 100,000 cars next month. Also, you don't dare to make 100,000 cars in a month after three months. Because you don't know if you can sell 100,000 cars later. This is a rule of the car itself.

But I think it's different for the machine. The machine may do a very good job at a certain point. I remember it's a point, not a surface. I think it may have a very big explosion effect. Just like I think the biggest one last year was the coding of Soapy. It's a very short time, less than 18 months. From the first half of the year to the end of last year, there was a huge change. I think it's possible for the machine to arrive. Although it's slower than it is, because it's still a digital world and a physical world, but it's still possible.

Will it be slower than a car?

I think if a machine can reach the hardware capacity, the hardware speed will be far above a car. Of course, let's change the perspective. Because the company is a car manufacturer, we are 80% self-employed. Our hands, chips, joints, we all work in self-employment. It's just that we may be working with a lot of companies in the tier 1, we may be working with the tier 2 that is more superior to it. Only in this way can you do the quality and scale well.

二十、量产难度与"iPhone 1 的时刻"

What are the difficulties of mass production in the second half of this year?

There are many. First, can we be reliable and stable in hardware? Second, can we make good integration with high-level models? Third, can we prove the business? There are countless holes in the machine.

So why do you say it's more difficult than a car?

As long as you build a car, if there is no end product, if there is a need for a user, you must believe that it is a thing that can be sold. But the machine, you don't dare to say. The machine has not been defined clearly to this day. The market is also waiting. The market is waiting to see if you have a similar, maybe like an iPhone 4, phone.

But I believe the first commercial mass-produced machine, I don't think it can reach the iPhone 1. But iPhone 1 is still a tool. It can be replaced. Because in that era, there were many other mobile phones. There was Nokia, Motorola, and Sony. But when the machine was first released, many people didn't do anything similar. I think it's not something that can be completely avoided.

How big do you think your victory is in such a long-term war?

It depends on how big your goal is. To know how big your victory is. I think, I think we probably have a two layers of victory. I think this is the highest victory I have seen in Chinese companies.

You told me that you were making cars and swimming in the blood. What about being a robot?

We are not in the blood yet. The blood is still ahead. But I think there is a big difference between robots. The AI in robots is very capable. The group of people who swim in the blood, the people who swim with you, in fact, most people don't know AI very well. Today, many people in the automotive industry say, "Because I don't know how to use software, I'll find a good T1." It works in the car, but it doesn't work in the machine. So the robot's blood and sweat, just like the Internet competition back then, will soon flow from the blood and sweat to the blue sea. So its progress will be very fast.

二十一、GX：汽车、飞行汽车、机器人能力的融合

We were talking about robots earlier. You will be releasing many new cars this year, including your GX. Let's talk about your product.

Yes, GX is Xiaopeng's first high-end product. This is our first full-size, large SUV with six seats. And our flagship is called the flagship SUV. It's very interesting. There are about 30 Beijing car shows this year.

I think we are thinking about how to integrate the capabilities of the car, the flying car and the machine into the new generation of autonomous vehicles. Let me give you an example. We have integrated the capabilities of the flying car into the new generation of autonomous vehicles. and the core components are brought into this test.

Of course, this GS is the first robot test in China. It has eight fully safe robots. That is, you drive it out of the field, the power is broken, but it can still be turned on. The power is broken, but it can still be turned on. The mouse bites the wire, but it can still be turned on. It's like an airplane. It allows you to have a robot.

I think the second one, for example, we connect the control chassis of the car with the new EEA system and the automatic driving of VIA. It can help the new chassis to improve the lower line during the VIA implementation. In other words, the safety level is higher, the time limit is shorter, and the degree of control can be increased by several percent. This is an example we gave.

The third one is that we will soon introduce, for example, this car, its third row space can be completely flattened into a two-seater or into a 2.5-seater car. We call it a car that can be made of four, five, or six seats.

So in fact, you will see that it has put a lot of Xiao Peng's own abilities, many abilities of Benchit, and many abilities of G-Chan, and made a very interesting fusion. and also added many of our partners such as the food glass we made with Flio and the new generation of car refrigerator we made with Meili.

I think there are many interesting combination of capabilities. So we always think that making a car with a big appearance and a good look and similar to the original and the details are not used and then we have made a lot of capable cars. I think it's that's called bloodshed. In some ways, it's actually seen in many tests.

But how can you make it different? It requires a full-scale study. Every point. Some people say my point is 60 points, that point is 80 points, and another point is 85. Maybe your worst point is 80 points. And then there are more 90% and 95%. You are likely to be in the middle of this war. So this is a very interesting thing.

Q：定价是怎么定的？

How is this price set? Did you think about it at the beginning or at the end?

No, I thought about a logic at the beginning. It must be a plan. This plan depends on what market you want to eat. What size do you want this market to have? What kind of profit do you want? And then what kind of profit.

Q：担心老款回头看吗？

Are you worried about the return of the old and the new?

I'm not worried. Because the thinking and logic at that time were very different from the thinking and logic at that time. There are many things that, in the next few years, if your ability reaches a higher level, you can analyze the previous problems in a general way. And you can ask the current problems in a general way. And we are not like before today. That is to say, the stronger your system is, the harder you are to figure out the problems at one or two points.

二十二、用户画像与朋友的反馈

You said you like this kind of car and you want to build a car for your friends. What kind of people are they? Who is your target?

I think people over 30 years old are more brave. Many people do a good job. Some are entrepreneurs, some are good managers, good professionals. They are willing to explore new technologies. Experience the good and bad of new technology. I think it is our first batch of users with a logic similar to our taste. So we expect this car to be better for the family.

What feedback do you have from your friends?

Many of my friends like it. I have a group of friends in Beijing tomorrow. They all come together to see our car. Many people told me, they said, "Children, you should have made such a big car long ago." He said, "I really want to be with my family."

I think we all have our own friends and allies, and we might be competitors. Today I went to his exhibition with my ideal, and I saw the new L9. I believe it's a very good car. Yesterday I went to Li Bin's exhibition and saw the ES9. I think that in the wine industry, we will give different views and opinions.

What did you make from the wine and use it in this generation of vehicles?

I think there are too many. Our organization has adjusted, our product planning has adjusted, our customer opinion has adjusted. 商业的逻辑也调整了,我觉得太多了。 这已经隔了三年半了吧,从2022年底到现在,我觉得太多了。

这么自信。

我觉得这是完全不一样的能力级, 汽车是一个非常复杂的技术的能性的经营的大集成体系。 所以为什么他说是制造的民族,所以…… The deeper you learn and summarize in this field, you will see many different points. In fact, they can't see it in many small or consumer components. They have handed out many manufacturing products. They have a lot of supply capabilities and supply chain. Its combination is unlikely.

So in the automotive field, why do I think from 2022 to 2026, Yes. You have to be number one in many fields to be able to do it. Density is one of them.

Why don't you choose a strategy that makes less money? Why do you want to do four cars this year?

I think every company has its own way. I don't think you can ask why A company doesn't make less money and B company doesn't make more money. There are different logics.

You seem to be very confident this year.

I think there's nothing untrustworthy about being an entrepreneur.

二十三、CEO 的时间分配与"成功"的定义

Where do you spend most of your time? In cars, robots, AI, or where? In organizations? Or in purchasing? You mentioned that everyone is involved, but I don't think so.

I think the most I think is in strategy and planning. What is the future like? Just like you asked a lot of questions just now, who is successful, etc. Maybe they will be more successful in the future. But from my point of view, they are not successful. Just like the size of a car, is it successful? It's one of the representatives of success, not necessarily all of them. Is the profit successful? Some of the profits may be temporary, which is painful. So the concept of success among entrepreneurs is very different.

So in another perspective, I may have spent more time thinking about the entire enterprise, the product, the business strategy and the planning issues. But these planning issues require your technical problems. your organization, your entire market, and other series of combination capabilities.

What is the most important thing for you this year?

None. All of them are important. Many of them are important. In fact, the car is a very complex problem. It's better than a personal or a clear enterprise saying, "I just need to do this one thing well." If the car does one thing well, it doesn't mean it can be done well. This is a very painful problem. Like I said, the problem of long and short drives and wide drives are the same. Many people think that you should make a long drive. In most cases, it's right. But in most cases, this problem is answered in the car. It's a compound machine. It's useless if you make one.

For example, you make the automatic gearbox very well. Make it level 4, OK? First, generally, I think, who said that I can do it today? I think it's a bunch of cowards. Second, I think even if he did it, it doesn't have a huge value to it. Because its value is gradually being reflected.

二十四、L4 的时间表与销量变化

When do you think L4 will be realized?

I think it will be in 18 to 24 months.

Is this for you or for the whole industry?

I think for us.

What will it bring in the future? Will it bring more sales?

More sales is 100%.

How much more sales?

I need to verify. Even if there is more sales, it does not mean long-term value. You see, there are different questions here. For example, last month, at the end of March, we released the first version of the second generation of VLA. I recently shared the data with you at the factory. In April, the sales of Chinese cars dropped by about 20%. We have increased by about 50% to 70%. There are some parts related to the second generation VLA. I think it's good.

If we make L4, how many times can we increase this capability? One times? Five times? Ten times? I think it's hard to judge whether it's good or bad. Secondly, what is the impact on society and the industry?

Today, in fact, we have many different views on this. I still want to say that the final success of a car company is not only based on one ability in AI, or one ability in hardware, or one ability in sales. I don't think so.

二十五、与余凯之争：自研 vs 第三方供应

Mr. Yu Kai often says that you are his most difficult user. He thinks that the car should be used by the third party. What do you think of his point of view?

Yu Kai is very close to us. I think he is walking on a very interesting and hopeful path, but also a very challenging path. It depends on whether there are more or less car engineers in the world. The more, the wider the road to success. The less, the more painful the road to success.

Which do you personally think?

I think it's getting more and more concentrated.

If we look at it from the perspective of the user, because we are looking at it from the perspective of an enterprise, the user perspective is probably one of the dozens of perspectives we have. So from the user's perspective, we all think that there needs to be more cars, more products for the consumer, more choices, more different things.

But in this world, cars are too complicated. If it's a simple thing, I think it will soon be back to normal. What I mean by back to normal is that Apple phones, I think, this is not a good word to say, in recent generations, Apple phones have not changed much, but they are still very stable. The change in the entire mobile market, now I buy a phone,

I think in the past, many car companies did not self-report. They said, "You go to these car companies, they did a lot of research, they spent a lot of money, they have a lot of people." I said, "But they don't self-report, they do grassroots." So you say they do grassroots is not right, you self-report, you will find that self-report is even more wrong. Because the car is too complicated, you have a lot of people who are also developing.

So you might as well say, "Some places you don't have a fund, you might as well find a partnership, find a partnership." That's right. But from another perspective, it depends on whether it knows your car's strategy or tactics. If it knows tactics, you shouldn't support it. If it knows strategy, you should support it.

Back to the end of the question, the question you asked at the beginning. For example, after 10 years, will our software stand up to the value of cars? If it does, will one or two tier 1 cars help our partners to reach 50%? Of course, he hopes so. I think maybe it can, maybe it can't.

We have a thing called cross-referencing in the car. It's a combination of many capabilities. There are hardware, software, manufacturing, design, service, and operation. These are all completely different. Then you merge them together. Many people say, how can this be integrated? For example, why do you hear a lot of "mixing and uniting" in the car? It's just putting different things together.

Why do they have so many different types of integration in the software? N integration, it may integrate multiple types of capabilities. The entire system is different. It's not a company that can do it. For example, you used to be able to cooperate with three companies to provide a total solution together. It's easy. But sorry, you have to cooperate with 300 companies to provide a personalized solution. It's very painful.

I think the road of Xiaopeng and Yukai's land-based line road may be successful. I don't know today. I think Yukai's land-based line road, the company did well, the product did well. I agree with this. But I still want to say that the ultimate strategy of this road, or the ultimate is what? How many cars or robots will be concentrated in the end? How long will it take? I think the difference between different companies is too big.

I won't talk about the foundation. I've been saying that in 2030, China may have five large-scale car companies. It doesn't mean that other car companies will collapse. But I think the decline in scale will make it harder and harder to enter this super-high-intensity competition.

二十六、是否游出血海

Did you swim out of the sea of blood today?

I think I'm swimming. I think... Did you come out? No, I don't think there's any. In my definition, first of all, don't ask me what my definition is. I don't think any of them is true.

The robot challenger is ten times better, right?

I think robots are better. Because the possibility of the robot being "diverted" will be low. The value of software will be very high. Everyone has no doubt about this. Secondly, I don't think there is a very good source that can help a computer company to make good software.

二十七、日常、读书与"后悔"

What is your typical day like?

I think it's probably the same as many entrepreneurs.

So what books do you read?

I don't read specific books now. I have a lot of books on my desk. I might just flip through one and that's it. I think the summary of the books 规律在现在的组合里面变化太快, 它很难组建。 当他写好一本书印刷出来之后, 这个世界可能又变化了10% 在他的那本书的范围内 假设。

所以我觉得 看书是应该在一个更稳定的环境去看。 怎么吸收知识? 吸收知识是最简单的。 我觉得是在实践中间吸收。 以前我自己总结吸收知识, 读万章书, 行万里路, 聊万次天。 然后 No.

Why do you regret?

结语

今天的节目就是这样。 这里是商业访谈录,是一档由语言及世界工作室出品的深度访谈节目。 你可以到公众号关注我们的工作室获取更多的信息。 我们的公众号是语言及世界,languageisworld。 也欢迎你在小宇宙的评论区与我们有更多的互动。 我们希望和你一起从这里探索新的世界。

观看原视频 →
item.description:
## 一、"AI 缝合怪"的自我否定：当成功本身成为最大的认知牢笼
何小鹏在这次访谈中最反常的动作不是"下了更大的赌注"，而是**主动停掉了一个花了几年、几十亿建成的成熟系统**。他把它命名为"AI 缝合怪"——一个用软件方法论 + AI 工具箱组合而成的"更强力的软件"。
意外之处在于：**这套被废弃的系统在行业内仍然是"先进"的**。它的"上限 1000 分、下限 900 分"的稳定性，正是大多数车厂梦寐以求的工程指标。何小鹏放弃它，不是因为它失败，而是因为它**成功得太合理**——合理到看不见 L5 的天花板。
这里隐藏着一个反直觉的系统性真相：**对于物理 AI 这种长周期赌博，工程上的"局部最优"恰恰是战略上的最大陷阱**。这与生物进化中的"局部适应陷阱"完全同构——一个物种在某个生态位适应得越完美，跨越生态位的成本就越高。何小鹏在 2022 年给自己立的两条座右铭"永不放弃 / 想赌就赌"，本质上是在对抗"成功路径依赖"这种最隐蔽的认知重力。
## 二、Token 的"两个宇宙"：物理 AI 与数字 AI 的不可通约性
整段访谈里有一个被他轻描淡写、但极具颠覆性的

item.title: [Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] He Xiaopeng: Robot IRON's Birth, The Accident, GX, His Big Bet & Swimming in Blood
item.dc:creator: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.content:encoded:
💡 创造性洞见

一、最反常识的发现：何小鹏在"用人脑的算力"做最贵的赌注

你注意到一个奇怪的悖论了吗？整场对话里，何小鹏反复强调一件事："我自己不用 AI Coding"、"很多计划不会跟人讨论"、"任何角度都只代表一个朋友的思考"。一个把 AI 押注到底层的人，自己的决策过程反而是反 AI 化的——高度依赖直觉、单点判断、不可复述。

这背后藏着一个隐秘的认知：在物理 AI 这种没有先例可学的领域，CEO 的核心价值不是"决策正确率"，而是"承担孤独的能力"。当你能讨论的人都只看到局部，讨论本身就变成噪声放大器。所以他的工作方式越来越像围棋顶尖棋手——不复盘、不解释、靠直觉落子。这与他大谈"AI 重塑组织"形成的反差非常有意思：组织被 AI 化，但决策的最高节点反而越来越像前 AI 时代的工匠。

二、跨领域连接：物理 AI = 缝纫机时代向纺织厂时代的跃迁

何小鹏反复用"缝合怪"（Stitch Monster）来描述传统智能驾驶。这个词其实精准击中了一个工业史的隐喻：

  • 18 世纪英国的缝纫机改良了"做衣服"，但真正改变世界的不是缝纫机，是后来的纺织厂——一种全新的组织+动力+流程范式。
  • Waymo 做了 17 年自动驾驶，本质是把规则系统"缝得更精细"，相当于"更高级的缝纫机"。
  • 何小鹏押注的第二代 VLA 是停掉缝纫机、重建工厂——这就是为什么他说"不是道路，是小道"。

更意外的连接是：他把"老人"作为机器人的第一终极用户，几乎所有同行都在讨论工厂、家务、商用。这是一个被严重低估的洞察——机器人最大的市场可能不是替代劳动力，而是替代"被需要感"。中国进入老龄化社会，社会关系网络快速塌缩，老人和机器人之间不是"工具关系"，而是唯一依赖关系。这本质上是把物理 AI 接进了人类的情感供应链——而这恰恰是数字 AI（ChatGPT）做不到的。

三、隐藏的系统性真相：他在赌"赔率"而不是"概率"

整场对话最容易被忽略的一句话："创业的乐趣，不就是在你当时能力下，赌你 10 倍到 1000 倍赔率吗？"

注意他的用词——赔率（odds），不是概率（probability）。这是一个量化交易员的思维框架，而非传统制造业 CEO 的语言。

延伸出去会发现一个更深的结构： - G9 是 10 倍赔率赌注（小幅创新、可能失败） - VLA 第二代是 1000 倍赔率赌注（停掉花了几十亿的旧系统，重建底层） - 机器人是 10000 倍赔率赌注（市场都还没定义清楚）

随着公司变大，他主动调低单次赌注的概率，但放大赔率——这其实是凯利公式（Kelly Criterion）在企业战略里的应用。绝大多数 CEO 越大越保守、追求高胜率小回报；何小鹏走的是相反路径。他自己说的 20% 胜率，配合 1000 倍赔率，期望值是 200 倍——这不是制造业的算盘，是对冲基金的算盘。

一个意外的推论：所谓"血海"其实不是问题，他真正担心的是"温水池"——一个胜率 80%、回报 1.5 倍的稳定市场。在那种市场里，他的赌徒型 CEO 能力会彻底失效。所以他不停地把自己往更难的赛道上推，本质上是在保护自己的生态位。

📝 转录文本

开场金句

"CEOs in the physical world don't dare to bet. I may be bolder. We made a huge bet. We stopped the previous system. That system cost billions."

"Intuitively speaking, what is the shortcoming of He Xiaopeng's skill? I think everyone becomes skilled, everyone will find that this person actually has many shortcomings."

"What does the robot mean to Xiaopeng? I think there may be many solutions in this world and we chose a humanoid robot that is the most human-like."

"What do you think your winning rate is? We have about 20%."

"In such an era of drastic technological changes, are you anxious? Of course you are anxious. Because you will discover a lot of your previous logic theory, even the theorems and paradigms at the bottom of your logic theory, even down to the axiom level — your values, outlook on life, and world view…"

"Have you swam out of the sea of blood today? I think they are all swimming. I don't think anyone has come out now."

Hello everyone, I am Xiaojun. "Unfinished Date" is a first-class program by Weibo Finance and Language is the World Studio co-produced interview program. Our guest today is He Xiaopeng, founder and CEO of Xiaopeng Group. Let's talk about his exploration of physical AI. But he still has many secrets that cannot be said.

一、AI 使用习惯：CEO 该不该亲自下场？

Before the show officially starts, I would like to first do a little research. What are the AI products you have used most recently?

Then I really don't have much. Still working on the very traditional AI products — Qianwen, Doubao.

But what about coding?

We use it a lot within the team now. But personally I don't want to use it.

Why?

They asked me: Xiaopeng, you have to use it yourself. I gave a very interesting example. I said when we were making Internet products, if you use the product every day, you will soon get to the details. For example, you will find that skills (functions) are not useful. If you understand it, you will think about it and think that its advantages will be very long in the future — there are many. But once you use it you will see its shortcomings and problems. Then you will focus on how to solve it. For example, a series of problems such as multi-person collaboration coding issues — they do not allow you to look into the distance.

So I am very concerned about the rapid changes in technology. Many product capabilities, I think, don't use it too deeply — especially for the No.1 position. But at the grassroots level, or there are some product lines that are strongly related to it, you have to encourage it. You can use any method. In the end it depends on the result. Then slowly normalize it. This is better.

AI coding for the No.1 position of an enterprise — does it make any sense? Do you think it will be good for Xiaopeng, the automobile industry or the intelligent driving industry? Will it bring any changes?

I think it is very good for junior programmers. It is still an auxiliary tool for now. But maybe in two to three years we will force junior programmers to move up to senior programmers.

But I think that for things like intelligent assisted driving or other strong AI capabilities, I think its help is relatively small. It is just one of the tools. The entire infra (infrastructure) must be built. Once the entire system is established — in fact, in itself, you can think — it helps everyone at the most application layer. That is AI coding. But if it is at the kernel layer, for example if you want to write an operating system, I think the core of it is the entire infra rather than coding.

二、Token 经济学：数字 AI 与物理 AI 的根本分歧

How many tokens do you use every month? How many tokens does the company use?

We don't pay much attention to this matter. In fact, there are many people involved in this matter mentioned in the past year.

I think digital small and medium-sized companies — there are a lot of things to mention. I think it should be particularly important. I think digital medium and large companies — pay appropriate attention, it should not be too comprehensive. Because some companies' digital support business — it may not necessarily be digital, there may be non-digital.

A very interesting one is: how many Tokens are needed in non-digital companies? Look at our car. We made an interesting little statistic before — our new generation of VLA, if it only uses three or four hours a day, how many Tokens will be used? I don't quite remember the exact number now. Of course, it's just an inner loop.

You can think. Let's change the perspective. In fact, that means in digital AI, the number of Tokens that can be used by AI is far lower than in the physical world — the number of tokens that the AI needs to use. But actually this is meaningless. Because it does not use humans. Use AI to meet his needs. Self-driving cars — you can think of it as an automatic machine. It uses tokens as many times as it wants.

So I am in these two fields. I think they are two different perspectives. So in the physical world, I think the use of Token in the future will be a machine — how many tokens will you use, how much value will it generate to the machine and people? And how many Tokens can be used to generate value for people and enterprises is another dimension. So its indicators should be different.

Its North Star indicator — what do you think is a reasonable amount?

I don't know. Internally, I try not to control tokens for everyone. Many people ask about not controlling tokens — he cost down a year's expenses in one quarter. I think if he can really do it, then the most important thing for me is to manage the most abnormal ones — ten situations in the TOP10. I think the others can be opened up. Because you are not sure: a person spends one thousand RMB a month, ten thousand RMB — is still the most valuable. And the monthly salary of each of us — it is very likely that it will far exceed this number. If he really has the ability to spend more money to generate greater value, why do you want to restrict him?

Where is the company's Token distribution now?

I think the main one is GIC (General Intelligence Center). In fact, it is the merger of our autonomous driving and the cockpit team. Their team is large.

I think Token is a term for an internal employee of a company. We have a certain perspective — can you tell me how many H100s were used? A piece of H100 — I don't know how many tokens I can use in a day. I haven't converted this into a ratio yet. Then what we calculate is, for example, I will give you 30,000 H100 or 50,000 H100 — what business do you use, able to be used efficiently and effectively inside this. Let's take a look at the usage of this computing power. I don't actually care much about the usage of Tokens.

You just mentioned outliers. What kind of outliers are there?

Let me give you an example. We recently focused on data (data) — made a whole control. Many people talk about the value of data. I think there are very few companies now seeing the huge cost of data. Because the amount of data in the digital AI field is very small. Dozens of TB can be used for training. When we train the data once — dozens of TB to hundreds of TB.

So how to manage data? Used storage is a huge money. Maybe we invest in data every year, all from direct rigid costs, both are close to more than 1 billion. So which data is valuable? What data is temporarily valuable? What data needs to be used very quickly? Which data can be warmed up and reused? The amount of money used per — they are all in the tens of millions. Then you can come and analyze it, then optimize. There are actually huge efficiency gains that can be made — logic that reduces costs and improves efficiency.

In fact, the same is true for computing power. Our data — there are different teams for computing power. Take care of this matter specifically. But we don't care too much. I see a lot of companies saying yes — the number of Tokens used by the company's engineers. I think that amount, the number of Tokens used by the machine and physical world AI models, the number of tokens required during training — those are very small numbers.

三、"何小鹏 Skill"：CEO 会被技能化吗？

If you were trained to be a He Xiaopeng skill, what training data do you think should be provided to it? What would that skill be like?

I think digital models and physical models — today we still let basic white-collar workers… it is easier to become skilled than basic blue-collar workers. If I could have a clear logic now — what does deskilling mean? It means not just basic blue and white-collar workers. It's the higher-end blue and white-collar workers. There will be huge risks. CEO can be replaced. Maybe in a few decades or a hundred years. At that time, my ability could indeed be skillsed. But at that time, maybe each of our CEOs will also have stronger, more comprehensive capabilities. He may have lost ABC but got new DEF.

Because your question is something we have not only thought about — we have thought deeply about. Because we are doing a lot of robotic things. Because actually changing the angle, I think it's in this — in fact, it has two very important contradictions. I think for most people will feel: how I put me, or turn his abilities into skills.

But from a model perspective, how did I know you were skilled? This ability is a right ability. And constant OL (continuous learning). For example, in a physical simulation model can be strengthened — actually in this it is related to coding. Or it's different from autonomous driving — coding and autonomous driving, it is relatively clear. I know what's wrong, right. But for example, let's make He Xiaopeng skillful. It's hard for you to judge why this skilling is right or is wrong. It is very difficult. So in such a system — it's us who are building the system, rather than using a system built by others. From my perspective these are two completely different angles, actually.

Intuitively, what is the shortcoming of He Xiaopeng's skill? Defect?

I think after everyone becomes skilled, will all find this person actually has a lot of shortcomings. And yours — of course there are.

四、AI 含量与公司定位：从智能汽车公司到物理 AI 公司

As a CEO, do you think your AI content today is enough?

I have no idea. I think in many companies in the digital world, I think it might be possible to put the content of AI into a few dozen percent. I don't think it should be done in the physical world. Xpeng is a company with tens of thousands of people. I think 15%–20% should be spent on AI. And the AI I'm talking about is pan-AI — including our autonomous driving, including our robots. We think this is considered the entire category of AI. I think it's enough.

Then I really want to discuss a topic with you today. That is, if artificial intelligence companies are on the side, manufacturing companies are on the other side — that's how your balance should swing. Do you have any tangles or pulls inside? Do you choose to be an AI company? Or be a manufacturing automobile company?

I don't understand it this way yet. Feel sorry.

I think of cars as having several kinds of research and development. I think the first one is hardware research and development. The second is software research and development. And AI is just one of them — one of the software. I think there is another one called manufacturing research and development. What you just said, Xiaojun — hardware R&D, they are two different abilities from manufacturing. For example, you have the ability to design a beautiful table and chairs, but you probably can't make it. These are two different abilities actually. So I think that in an automobile company, in fact, the research and development of these four abilities is a foundation. There may even be a fifth type in the future. This is my opinion.

You were still talking about it last year — you are an AI car company. This year, Xpeng Motors just completed its name change, it's called Xiaopeng Group. You said you are a physics AI company. What was the reason behind this name switch? From an AI car company to a physical AI company — what are its changes?

I think this change has just begun. There are still many things that cannot be clearly described. But I can say something.

For example, in the past — Xpeng Motors' previous decade, in the middle of entrepreneurial career, in fact, it is doing the core three words and six words — that is, smart electric vehicles. So in the past ten years of entrepreneurship, we made our first car and then mass-produce it, sold the first 100,000 units, then make a second car, and so on. It will enter a cycle like this.

But actually 12 years ago — that is when Xpeng Motors started its business in 2014 — basically no one believes in intelligence. But there are also a few people who believe in electrification. More people believe cars are big business. 2025, that is, last year, everyone believes that electrification is the future. Because everyone sees China and sees the world — a change in new energy.

So in the past few years, in fact, although intelligence is developing very fast in the automotive field, but actually I think the development is also very unsatisfactory. Because there have been many companies since earlier times — from Toyota to Google to China's Baidu, Tesla the overseas car manufacturer, like China's Xiaopeng, etc. We are all driving autonomously. Did a lot of things. From a certain angle, I think it achieved the effect but not enough height.

That is to say — at that time, I thought it was still an AI algorithm plus the combination of software rules capabilities. I nicknamed it the Stitch Monster. Not actually designing robots with the entire AI driver. Nor is it the ability to use the entire AI model to come and build this system. It's still software-based logic.

五、第一代 VLA vs 第二代 VLA：停掉花了几十亿的旧系统

Xiaopeng last year — we feel that we have also encountered a very big change. That is to say, we were working on two generations at the same time — new intelligent assisted driving. Internally called the first generation of VLA, second generation of VLA.

第一代 VLA — in fact, in the past end-to-end field: enlarge it, enlarge the model, lower the rules of software, enhance the usage scenarios and scope, enhanced backend capabilities. For example, strengthening — for example, post-training. This is a direction.

But actually another direction is to say: how do I throw away the original one? End-to-end logic. In fact, a larger foundation model is used on more thoughts. How to open the upper limit of autonomous driving first — then go to the lower limit of convergence.

In fact, the lower limit of convergence means fewer errors as per your agreement — be able to act according to your expectations, able to exercise. But in fact its generalization ability — there are many places where things are done poorly.

For example, give an example: today there is no software from any self-driving company — ability to drive smoothly in underground parking lots. All parking lots operate what is called memory-assisted driving. In fact, he opened it once — it knows where your parking spot is, what is your approximate driving route. In fact, its understanding of the entire physical world is very low. The core is still not smart enough. Its upper limit is too low.

You can think — you have to open up to 10,000 possibilities to do it well. But they're probably all around a thousand points. I'm just a hypothesis. So change the angle. It was around this time last year, I guess — our other generation VLA, I think it opened me up to a new change. I think its upper limit might be — it can range from 100,000 points to 1 million points. But the lower limit at that time was also very tragic. Originally you expected to make a product, the upper limit is 1000 points, the lower limit is 900 points — your ability at this time is pretty good. But at that time, the lower limit may only be 100 points. So it is better than other — the lower limit of our own products is lower. Then it works. There are so many questions.

But we ended up making a really huge bet. Let's just say that in the past, software engineering was used — the process of using software, plus AI algorithms and AI tools, including the coding you just mentioned. You can think of it as being in a business flow, or software. In some important tool links and process links, it's using AI. But we think it's wrong. Is this still sewing? Software it makes. Or software. Because you are using software methodology — toolbox for using AI. The result is a more powerful software. I think it's called AI Stitch Monster.

We made a huge transformation and gamble last year. We stopped the previous system. That system cost several billion.

What time last year? Probably now — what is the reason?

I don't think it can be driverless. I don't think it's possible either. Let robots truly generalize. For example, we arrived at an unfamiliar venue today. The robot can come over and say "Hello Xiaojun", then sit down, then you say "I won't hand you a glass of water", then it says "Yes I don't need it". I think it might be a strong rule plus minority algorithm — an ability to use AI.

At that moment we judge that we are talking about ourselves — in fact, smart electric cars. What we expect is a very smart car. But the method we use is will never be infinitely smart.

六、为什么直到去年才看清？数字 AI 与物理 AI 的方法论鸿沟

Why did I only realize it last year? Xiaopeng has been in business for 11 years, right? You are also very early in developing intelligence.

I think most of them are not from the perspective of physical AI — go and see something. I think digital AI can change a lot of things. I think with physics AI and changing a lot of things are two completely different methodologies with different paths.

But most people, or look at digital AI from a scientific research perspective? Digital AI actually has some perspective. It's in human language. Physics AI does not use human language. Digital AI is human language — is highly summarized and condensed. That's right. So language is the world. But in the physical world, the amount of data each of us sees every day — it is simply impossible to summarize, describe, restore and copy in words. It's too big.

Change a different angle. One of our former CEOs in the automotive field — it's easy to use AI in the digital world. After understanding it, I feel I seem to understand its logic. I seem to understand its logic — go map it to extend and use. But at that time, no CEO would say I want to use them all. Because I don't know. How effective is it really?

Because you need to know — in the digital AI market, many models just run and compare scores. I think that's ridiculous in physics AI. Because it is not only higher than the upper limit. For example, running a score — it is also lower than the lower limit. It must maintain the lower limit. It also requires breadth. For example, your quality, your cost, your material, your details, what your policies and regulations allow. This is all called shortcomings.

Long board and narrow board. Make narrow boards wider. Make shortcomings longer. Longboards need to be longer. So in the digital world, the core thing to look at is the longboard. I don't really read the other two boards. So the CEO of the physical world would not dare to bet otherwise. Otherwise, I think I still have a lot of boards. What should I do?

I think I might be bolder. I was at that time — I don't know if it can solve its shortcomings. Can you make my narrow board wider? We made a huge bet. We just think — I believe in physical AI. Change your thinking. Change the process. The bottom layer needs to be organized in a different way. It is possible to do something different.

So the path we take, like many large-model companies we see today, or some companies that apply the model, they all take completely different paths. So that's why I think Tokens are all application-based companies. What they care about — in fact, we have not been in this dimension for a long time.

Because when I was making smart electric cars in the past, I always feel that there is a very painful point. No matter how you look at it, hardware as a proportion of a customer's needs are far greater than 50%, right? I think if in the next ten years — very likely, hardware and software may each account for 50%. So we need to use AI — repower your organization, design the integration of your software and hardware, and then get greater value from AI. Only in this way is it possible in the new decade — comprehensive expressiveness and value of software, the value that users are willing to pay for it. Only then will it occupy, for example, a car, or 50% of a robot.

I think this is a very big change. So this is why Xiaopeng started to think about how to put physical physical — including cars, not just limited to cars — can be greatly integrated with AI. Our AI is actually about digital AI. I think basically they use — for example, Claude Code is very good, I will use Claude Code. If DeepSeek V4 can be very good in some aspects, and the cost is great too, then I'll switch that part over. We made a Hub. Can be easily switched. But in physical AI, the models of physical AI are different. The data of physical AI is different. The infra of physical AI is different. Then its final effect — if we could combine the three boards we just talked about, we can all fight well. It's possible to make a difference. This is a rough framework that we can talk about. I won't say any more.

七、去年四月发生了什么？

What happened in April last year? Why such a big bet was made last April? Did you receive any stimulation?

No. In fact, it's about experiencing different abilities. In March last year, I was thinking — why do all companies think with simpler methodologies and faster performance, can make one. It seems to be just about usable autonomous driving. It's right. But after one to two years of doing it, it will be found that in order to solve many shortcomings, it limits it to its own longboard. So it finds that it can never do level 4 or level 5.

This was an idea I had at the beginning of last year. It's just possible that this road is a shortcut. But it's not a road. It's a small road. We need to find a way — can really do it, maybe.

The 50:50 value of hardware plus software just mentioned — the value I talk about is not what technical people think about. Customers are willing to pay for it. Users are willing to say: I want to buy a car worth 300,000 yuan, 150,000 of which counts as your hardware, 150,000 is calculated as the comprehensive capability of your software. Is there such a car? Not now. But I think it will definitely change in this decade. So changes like physics and changes like AI — rethink. I think it's a very important point.

So this time our Xiaopeng is in the new decade — everyone will see. I think smart electric cars, maybe we will continue to mention, continue to be our core. But we no longer use electric — or think about intelligence the way cars do. But a different kind of logic. And all the businesses we think about — from planning, it should be called strategic planning — to the process of R&D, will be adjusted gradually.

Were there any landmark meetings in March and April last year within the company? You made this very big bet. First half of last year, it should be the time when Xpeng's sales pick up, right? Then start thinking more the direction for the next decade.

I don't think there is a meeting. Basically shot inside the head. Go all out.

I think it was probably at the end of the third quarter of last year. At the end of the third quarter, we made a very big move. We put the entire autonomous driving center — the core organizational structure has been completely changed. Each time window period — there are many outstanding classmates. But everyone has their original inertia. You will get used to using the old methods. Enjoy the latest tools and technology to make something better. I think most of the time it's right. But there are many times when your work methodology needs to be changed. So I think I need to adjust my methodology and my mindset.

八、AI 驱动的组织：为什么不能复制？

This sounds like a decision you made in your head within the company. There may not be a very iconic meeting or a node. But after forming this decision in your mind, what did you do next? This is what you call this AI-driven organization. Using AI to design integration of software and hardware. What to do next? For such a large organization, can you share your steps with us?

I can't say any of this. I'm sorry. These are what we are doing. Of course, it is also done in stages. I can only tell you — use different rhythms in different businesses. The core of the same business is the issue of cadres and organization. Actually it is a very difficult thing.

Why does it have to be driven by AI? What will the previous inertia cause?

In fact, you are just inside the logic of the software. Make a more complex stitch monster. Just like you build a house. You will use more materials and more crafts using AI. But it is still a methodology for repairing an old house. What it has renovated is still an original house. It's just that maybe it can be repaired faster.

We want something different. The most basic time — when there are many different things, everyone wants to see it. That's why he believed it. But as the CEO of a company, I think so. Sometimes you have to trust the nodes in the middle of the process. Want everyone to see — because it can improve confidence, can improve leadership. Many times it's because you believe in doing the layout.

But as a small company, it's different from a large company. Although Xpeng Motors is a startup company, but from a scale perspective, it's not too small in terms of staff size — tens of thousands. So how do you divide people like this into things? I think it's very different. Such an act — I'm having a hard time with physics AI, share for planning organizations.

So why did you ask me a question just this time? I said there are questions I can't answer. First we are trying to test. Second, any plans and goals — they will not be shared with others. Why there are so many things that don't need to be discussed? Because it's hard to have someone I can discuss it with you if it's accurate. You can only listen to the thoughts and opinions of many people — they're probably mostly wrong. Because they are not giving you advice from an overall perspective. All from a certain angle. But you have control of the overall situation. Each angle represents different friends and their own thinking logic. You have to bring it together. Finally you place the bet. Just like starting a business.

九、关于"赌"：永不认输 vs 愿赌服输

I heard you've become more willing to gamble in the past three years.

Yeah, that's not it either. I think it's just a lot of the time — know if and when you want to place a bet, it would be better to get off earlier. It's not that I'm more willing to gamble.

By the end of 2022, at that time, Xiaopeng encountered many challenges. At that time, I had two ideas for myself. It has something to do with gambling.

  • 第一个叫永不认输（Never admit defeat）。
  • 第二个是愿赌服输（Willing to admit defeat）。

If you take these two, they can all be put together better — to balance it. It means that even in the face of great difficulties, you have to persist. Maybe if you try harder it will pass. But you have to have a very good attitude.

Like what I just said — when I put the second generation VLA, I think it should be enlarged, and finally firmly put the first generation VLA, or even stop it completely. From business to technology, even to the organization, then a huge double psychological and material pressure. You at this time, the more you hesitate, the more you wait. The more I want to observe. The more you think about it, the harder it will be for you to succeed in another six months. So the judgment of measurement in this — it's really a very interesting point. But I believe that in a few years, maybe we can talk about it later when we are more successful.

十、每家公司的路径都不可复制

I feel that during today's process — this first: every company takes its own path. It cannot be copied. So I think today, China is talking about learning from Company A, learn from Company B — I think it's all wrong. Judging from our scale today to analyze and think — finding a path that suits you is the most important thing.

Everything done in the second digital AI — many cannot be copied to physical AI. This is a different world. Many people mention physics AI, I was laughing. I said first of all, have you ever done business in the physical world? If the business is not completed in the physical world, just from a digital world to analyze the definitions and laws of the physical world, to build an AI for the physical world — I think it is still a physical world in a narrow sense.

This physical world — there are also many things related to people. The environment and regulations of the physical world related to people. Finally there is business. It forms a thing. Which ones can go first? Which ones are behind?

Of course, from a scientific research perspective, you don't have to worry about it. But if you look at it from an engineering perspective, even jumping from the product perspective, jump to the product perspective again, jump to the product perspective of scale — you have to think broader and deeper. Rather than just the logic of two or three long boards.

So that's why I can't answer you. I think — you can go to any previous CEO of digital AI, they won't tell you. They think logically when transforming in AI. Because I don't know if I can succeed. Secondly, I won't share it with everyone.

十一、AI 帮企业的三件事 vs 物理 AI 的未知

But this is a common problem faced by everyone today. Is it right? That is, we must transform. I don't completely agree with this sentence. Where is digital AI today? Can get good transformation. What aspects are not used?

For a big company, if there are 10,000 people or 50,000 people, if the transformation is delayed by three days, the probability of success increases by 15%. To a microcompany — if he uses a homogeneous methodology today, homogeneous organization, it doesn't change, it can bet. Because it is small.

And today's AI can help us — nothing more than three kinds: - 第一种是简单分析员（simple analyst）。我现在很多地方不再用分析员了——AI 在帮我分析。 - 第二种是简单程序员（simple programmer）。 - 第三种是简单设计师（simple designer）。

In fact, it is mainly auxiliary — improved efficiency, improved performance. It does not replace. It also has no super large-scale synergy effect. It hasn't arrived at all. So change the angle. For small and micro companies in these three directions, I think the fit is better — should be faster. For those not related to these three directions, maybe a little later.

And for physics AI — today, no one has thought clearly at all. What is the position in the physics AI world? What I just talked about were three positions. What position is the easiest? Where is the highest priority to land? Most of what I see talks about subverting the world, using AI to improve efficiency — no concept at all. So I think it is — from some angles it's right. In some ways it is completely devoid of logic.

We actually feel that the physical world — many positions will change. Far beyond the digital world. Because in this world, it turns out that if we say 360 lines, there are 36,000 different positions. We feel that our role in the digital world is relatively limited. Then many people will not make this judgment? Many people say — aren't there more white-collar jobs than blue-collar jobs? There are many white-collar workers. It is also a position that integrates the digital world and the physical world.

Actually a certain angle — I think if we look at the physical world today, suppose I start by saying: Google bets on autonomous driving in 2009. It's now 17 years. I think Waymo is a solution that is both good and bad. Why? The good thing is that I think its technical capabilities are good. I think it's hard to globalize. I think in the past time, level 4 technology — it is naturally a more advanced suture monster. It's hard to be in AI, achieve extremely high-strength generalization. I don't think it's an extremely smart methodology. Of course, Google is now in the new big model did a lot of things. I think maybe they will change in the future. But I haven't seen it yet.

So change the angle. I just want to give a few examples and say: even if it makes it easier and safer for drivers, drive faster — each of us is a driver — just doing this thing for 17 years, no company can do a great job. Okay, change the angle. Many very small companies, several guns, more than a dozen people said that I want to use AI coding to change things. I think they completely underestimated it — the diversity and complexity of changes in the physical world. Just like we started with mobile Internet, rush into the automotive industry. They all feel that I have done something to the extreme. It would be nice to be more extreme. NO. Because you are considering the wrong dimensions. It is a physical dimension. You are a digital dimension. You achieve the ultimate in the digital dimension — its value point may be extremely small.

十二、用脚投票：变革中的反对声音

When you made this crucial bet last year, are there any negative voices within the company? Are there any objections?

That's for sure. I think most non-AI supervisors feel that it doesn't matter whether you do a or b. Maybe all wrong. Because at that time, their understanding of AI is not strong enough. I think most managers related to AI — some people think yes, some people think no, but most of them are in the intermediate state. I'm just not sure if it's a good rhythm. Not sure if this can achieve a landing effect. In fact, even I was the same at that time.

But that's the fun of entrepreneurship. Entrepreneurship for everyone — isn't it just based on your ability at the time? Bet you 10 times to 1000 times the odds? If you want to bet 1000 times, the probability of you being sorry is one in a thousand. If it is 10 times, your probability of success may be one in ten. So it's like the initial bet to start a business. As your startup grows in size, you also need to gamble. Just saying — at that time you should not bet 1000 times on probability theory. You may bet between several times and 10 times.

What's the biggest objection you've ever heard?

I think the biggest objection is many people vote with their feet. How to say. The meaning of voting with your feet is very simple — they don't believe this. They think they can't do it. He left. Do other things.

Today I think it is in a company — I think thinking about organization, remember not to chop down big trees with a knife. Cut slowly. Think it over and cut it off. So you dare to bet on certain things. From organization to process to direction. Change all. Change all. Right.

So the surgery last year was a big one, right? Yes. What everyone sees may be the top one. Business layer or application layer. But what you see from the inside is all the way to the root.

So I still want to say — how to use AI to drive certain businesses is very important. Instead of using hardware to drive the overall system. I hear a lot of your shows. Basically they are talking about digital AI right. Basically no one — in a crowd of tens of thousands, a large number of products that strongly interact with the real world — think about AI from a different perspective. What I want to say is: copy their methodology to physics AI — I think there are many places that are not suitable enough. Maybe some of the business logic is correct. But they are not comprehensive and sufficient enough. This is the biggest problem.

十三、AI 的四大方向

What should physics AI do? Promote it in a formed organization — I feel like I can't tell. Because I am also exploring from my perspective.

Today AI has four main directions. Everyone needs to explore.

  1. 数字 AI 如何变（How digital AI changes） — 很多人在思考和行动。
  2. 物理 AI（Physics AI） — 可能比数字 AI 难 100 倍。But I think starting in 2027 or 2028, everyone will see the effects of physical AI. Things like ChatGPT will start to appear. Huge changes in digital AI such as Anthropic. That is to say — which position has it been of great help to? There may even be some replacement in the long run.
  3. AI on the human body — Today no one really, apart from Google's AI on the human body, couple the two very well. Because the pharmaceutical industry is a very complex industry. Including pension. I think the human body is not just about internal circulation, there is also an external loop.
  4. 企业与 AI 的耦合（Enterprise–AI coupling） — 部门与 AI、中心与 AI 的耦合相对简单，但业务的耦合更难。But I see that there are many small businesses now — probably within 1,000 people. I think they're starting to explore more. I think this is a very good point. In fact, we are constantly observing and learning. Do the same at the right time.

So I think enterprise AI today — for small, medium and large companies — it's all an opportunity.

十四、CEO 的焦虑：从公理被颠覆开始

So now for companies like Xpeng, it can be considered a period of change, right?

Yes, and it's a long-term period of change. Technological changes may be completed in a month. Organizational changes — I'm not talking about a small organization. First of all, if it is a global medium-sized organization, I think three years have changed — it's already an extremely terrifying speed. I even think 5–10 years is already a fast speed.

In such an era of drastic technological changes, are you anxious? Be a CEO.

Of course anxious. How anxious? Because you will find a lot of your previous logic theory — even the bottom layer of your theory of logic, theorem, paradigm in your mind, even further down, maybe even to the axiomatic level. It's your values, outlook on life, and world view. You will all feel under the changes of these things — it seems that the previous set of logic did not work so well.

But how does it work? Remember: 看到问题、解决问题、构建系统——这是三种完全不同层次的能力。 Many people see the problem. This was when I didn't start a business — I also see numerous issues. When you are an entrepreneur, you find there are many problems — it should exist naturally. You can't solve the problem at all. You are a CEO — you can't solve it either. You are a bigger and more powerful person — you can't even solve it. Because of the problem you see — it's just a part of a certain angle of the world's problems.

So this is a very interesting topic. So the anxiety you just talked about — that is, you will constantly reconstruct your theorem, even axiom.

What kind of axioms have been overturned in the past? Is there anything that I believed in very firmly in the past? Today I feel like it doesn't work in this era?

Let me give you one that has nothing to do with this, but there are some related examples.

For example, when Xiaopeng launched autonomous driving — after doing it for many years, the more you keep doing it, the more you feel: it seems like level 5 will never be reached. Right? Because you think — use software in all scenarios around the world, regulations, man-machine (I am talking about man-machine — that man-machine should be called ergonomics — it's the interaction between people and the world). You will find that it loops infinitely.

But when you actually use AI to reconstruct this matter — do you think it might exist? When it may exist, you will face a lot of the original so-called barriers. Logic will generate new ideas. I can only click here. I won't go into details anymore.

十五、机器人的诞生：三个阶段

When it comes to AI, let's not talk about cars first. Because cars are a business that has been around for a long time. Let's talk about your robot first. Your robot was quite popular at home and abroad last year. Can you tell us how this product came about? I heard you are in 2023, is this the direction of universal humanoid robots?

Xpeng's robot is actually divided into three stages.

第一阶段：2018–2020。 It is an independent team. That time, same as many other robotics teams in China — you bought it, right? Right. At that time, there were about four or five companies doing four-legged work.

第二阶段：2020–2023。 It took us about three and a half to four years. Made three different milestones. I think this is the second stage. We have tried many in this. We use robot methods to make robots. Make robots like cars. I also did some stitching in the middle to make a robot. We all have different successes and failures.

第三阶段：2023 之后。 There will be a new logic. We see this new model in 2022. We think robots — it turns out we thought it was impossible to succeed in the brain. There is just no possibility of success with brains. Because the complexity of the cerebellum is actually much higher than that of everyone else.

Many people said today — the robot's cerebellum is ready. I said that's not the cerebellum. Walk forward slowly with the same monotonous pace — I don't think it's the cerebellum. That's the spine. Or your brainstem — is just maintaining balance. You are far from even having a cerebellum. They said it's also called the cerebellum. He said that your motor cerebellum is actually your brain. OK anyway, I think it's actually just different statistical logic.

So after 2023, Xiaopeng's robot re-enters a — from a firm start on four feet to two feet. Never trust the brain (just like I didn't believe in level 5 at that time), into a firm — a new design that drives robots from the brain. Then add what we see in the automotive field: good technology does not mean good products. Good product does not mean good product. Just because the product is good doesn't mean you can scale up. Because the car has a relatively complete set of — from planning to design, a complete process from ET to PT to SOP to SOD.

In fact, at the end of this year, we just hope to get into robots — car-like SOP. Next year 2027, it is very likely that the robot is on top of a high-level robot, entering the first year of commercial mass production. Whether it is China or the United States, will try.

I think in the past, the era of motion-controlled robots will appear with high-level robots, will gradually decline moderately. I think it will enter a stage of bifurcation.

Actually — in fact, everyone expects physics AI. The core representative robot in the middle — it can help humans. What kind of value is achieved? Able to work. This value used to be emotional value, right? Emotional value is valuable. But more importantly — a combination of physical value plus emotional value. So I think next year is the first possible year to start.

十六、为什么打散原班人马？

What happens in 2023 — let you change from not believing to believing?

2023, because I think at that time, if we use a robot that knows a lot about robots, very familiar team — incapable of making a good, a new generation of robots. LC was used at that time (米良川，小鹏机器人中心负责人). So we took a team of 300 people. Less than 60 people remained. We broke it up out. I heard they told me that there are 10 entrepreneurial teams. Most of them have gotten their money now. It's like Zhongqing has taken many rounds of money.

I think to reconstruct the logic of the entire robot — you can't use the pure car people you used to have. Nor can we use the pure robots from before. But you can't understand everything. So what kind of person should be used? I have no idea. So I picked a completely new team at that time. It also knows some AI. Learn more about cars. Know more about engineering. Learn more about robots. Then try to make one — brand new thinking about robot logic.

Why was he chosen at that time (LC)?

I think a lot of times it's fate. You can't say it's because he doesn't specialize in robots. He doesn't specialize in intelligent driving either. So I choose him, right? He's really neither. I thought at the time, his quadrant is more consistent with my thinking quadrant. So he's mine in the quadrant — one of several people thinking about options. First you choose him. He also wants to choose you. His reverse selection — I think you can try.

So I often see a lot of demos of robots outside, or demonstration or video. I said many of them are still with us, maybe on the third or fourth generation technology stack — go do testing. I myself said maybe he can succeed. But I tried. So I think Xiaopeng is in continuous innovation. We are very patient and courageous to invest a long time.

So today there are a lot of robotics companies. It makes a demo very quickly — it's nothing. If it comes to me, just like in 2017, at that time in China, there are many level 4 data companies. Just like today's robots. Of course there are more robots — does not represent these technologies finally be able to see the real value. Just a simple analogy. Because this kind of comparison is not good either.

You chose the person in charge of this robot. His subsequent selection criteria for you — has there been any impact? Do you think this is a method that can be continued? Because of what you said, I would think of saying Musk. He especially likes to use generalist people. He doesn't like to use expert people.

I think it might be in Tesla's Silicon Valley — it would be easier to find more generalists to choose from. I think in most countries, city or industry, is difficult.

I'd say this is the first: I think LC pays great attention to — his term is called the density of talents. I actually think it's the potential of the talent. He basically uses the best people. For example, from the end of last year to the first half of this year, we are probably similar to Sanqing level master's and doctoral graduates — just one department has hired nearly 80 people. They are all expensive. But we are all willing.

Then you need to be able to gather such a group of people. You must be willing to support their long-term exploration.
